name: Tornadizos de Ávila and Embalse de Becerril Loop
name_original: Tronadizos de Ávila y embalse de Becerril — circular desde Ávila
description: This moderate 23.1-mile mountain biking route from Ávila explores varied terrain, including the scenic Embalse de Becerril and fun singletra
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ávila offer diverse and rugged natural landscapes, particularly within the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. This region features high peaks, granite slopes, and varied terrain ideal for mountain biking. Cyclists can traverse dense chestnut, pine, and oak forests, encounter rivers and reservoirs, and ride through fertile valleys. The area provides a range of difficulties, from moderate to challenging, with significant elevation gains. Best mountain bike trails around Ávila The most popular mountain bike trail is Castro de las Cogotas MTB Loop from Ávila, a 27.2 miles (43.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic area of Castro de las Cogotas, offering a mix of cultural interest and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tornadizos de Ávila and Embalse de Becerril Loop, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path. This route features scenic views around the Embalse de Becerril reservoir and through the surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Arco de Consejeros – Los Perrillos loop from Ávila, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through diverse forests and open landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Ávila is defined by the granite slopes of the Sierra de Gredos, dense forests, and fertile valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Ávila?
A: There are over 100 mountain bike trails around Ávila, offering a wide variety of routes for different skill levels. The komoot community has mapped 113 tours in the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ávila?
A: Mountain biking in Ávila primarily features the diverse and rugged landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. You'll encounter granite slopes, dense chestnut, pine, and oak forests, as well as placid rivers, streams, and reservoirs. The terrain offers a mix of challenges, from technically easy gravel roads to steep sections with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for different skill levels in Ávila?
A: Yes, Ávila caters to various skill levels. While the majority of routes are moderate (85 tours), there are also 11 easy trails suitable for less experienced riders or those looking for a relaxed ride, and 17 difficult routes for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ávila?
A: The best times to explore the mountain bike trails in Ávila are generally in &lt;strong&gt;spring or autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural colors of the landscape are spectacular. While summer is an option, be aware that valleys can experience intense heat.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ávila?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Ávila are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084760287/castro-de-las-cogotas-mtb-loop-from-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Cogotas MTB Loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10413240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de Consejeros – Los Perrillos loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Ávila pass by historical and natural points of interest. You might encounter ancient sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5743721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Cogotas&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy scenic views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Canchal&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer glimpses of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain biking routes in Ávila?
A: While individual komoot tours are typically day rides, the Sierra de Gredos region is known for offering multi-stage journeys. Some routes can cover substantial distances, such as a four-stage journey of almost 225 kilometers (140 miles) with nearly 4,000 meters (13,123 ft) of elevation gain, providing options for extended adventures.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Ávila?
A: The mountain biking experience in Ávila is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse and rugged natural landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes in Ávila feature scenic views of reservoirs and rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087696453/tornadizos-de-avila-and-embalse-de-becerril-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tornadizos de Ávila and Embalse de Becerril Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers beautiful vistas around the Embalse de Becerril reservoir. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; on some trails.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that are close to the city of Ávila?
A: Absolutely. Many trails start directly from or near the city of Ávila, allowing for easy access. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls – Río Chico Park loop from Estación de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a route that incorporates urban and natural elements close to the city center.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Gredos is the defining feature of mountain biking in Ávila. Many trails, especially those with significant elevation, will provide stunning panoramic views of the high peaks and granite slopes of this impressive mountain range. The varied terrain ensures you'll be immersed in its natural beauty.
tips: Near Tornadizos de Ávila all those fans of mountain biking will find this short but fun "single track" that is worth adding to the route.
Beautiful ''single track'' between green crops leaving a picturesque picture worthy of a postcard.
Located in a beautiful spot near the town of Ávila, is the Becerril Reservoir. An ideal place to add to our BTT route.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: The Tronadizos de Ávila y embalse de Becerril circular route typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a varied terrain, characteristic of mountain biking in the Ávila region. You'll encounter paths through fields and potentially unpaved segments. A notable feature is the "short but fun" Singletrack of the Arroyo de Tornadizos, which adds an engaging technical element to the ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is classified as a moderate mountain bike route. It requires good fitness and some sections may demand advanced riding skills. While accessible to a broader range of mountain bikers, it offers a rewarding challenge beyond a beginner level.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse natural landscapes, including scenic views of fields and the beautiful Embalse de Becerril. The route also features a fun Singletrack Between Fields and the engaging Singletrack of the Arroyo de Tornadizos.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details are not provided, the Ávila region generally offers appealing conditions for outdoor activities. Consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter conditions that could affect trail quality.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific route is not available. It's always best to check local regulations and signage regarding pets in natural areas around Ávila before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking the Tronadizos de Ávila y embalse de Becerril route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Ávila, offering convenient access. While specific parking locations are not detailed, starting from Ávila implies various parking options within or on the outskirts of the city. It's advisable to check local parking availability in Ávila before your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Ávila, you will find a full range of amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, within the city itself. There are no specific amenities mentioned directly along the trail outside of Ávila.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Approximately 35% of the trail follows the Camino de Santiago del Sureste and Etapa 21: Cebreros - Ávila. Smaller portions also coincide with the Camino Viejo de Tornadizos.

faqs.question: What kind of fitness level is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: A good level of fitness is required for this moderate mountain bike route. With a distance of over 37 km and an elevation gain of nearly 570 meters, riders should be prepared for a sustained effort.

waypoints.name: Singletrack of the Arroyo de Tornadizos
